top_3_actions: Trust the car's grip — it has more than you're using. Carry more entry speed into corners, hold throttle longer on exit, and brake later/harder to spend more time near the grip limit.
[Turn_6] Begin progressive throttle application at or slightly before the apex. You do not need to wait until the car is fully straight. Target: throttle > 10% within 5-10m of the apex.
[Turn_2] Apply throttle more progressively on exit. Wait until the steering is unwinding before increasing throttle past 50%. Think: 'unwind the wheel, then add throttle' — not both at once.

verdicts.finding: Only 14.2% of cornering time near grip limit (peak: 1.99g, target: >25% of corners at ≥80% peak g).
verdicts.reasoning: During corners and braking, the car reaches 1.99g peak, but spends only 14.2% of that time above 80% of its own peak. This means most corners are being driven well below the car's capability — either entry speed is too low, apex is taken too cautiously, or exits are lifted early.
verdicts.action: Trust the car's grip — it has more than you're using. Carry more entry speed into corners, hold throttle longer on exit, and brake later/harder to spend more time near the grip limit.

verdicts.finding: Throttle applied 20m after the apex (apex at 2307m, throttle at 2327m).
verdicts.reasoning: The car covers 20m between the apex and first throttle application without accelerating. This costs approximately 3.5 km/h of exit speed that compounds down the following straight.
verdicts.action: Begin progressive throttle application at or slightly before the apex. You do not need to wait until the car is fully straight. Target: throttle > 10% within 5-10m of the apex.

verdicts.finding: Rear wheelspin detected on corner exit.
verdicts.reasoning: The rear tires exceeded their traction limit during acceleration. Spinning tires generate less forward force than rolling tires, so the car actually accelerates slower despite more throttle input. It also risks snap oversteer.
verdicts.action: Apply throttle more progressively on exit. Wait until the steering is unwinding before increasing throttle past 50%. Think: 'unwind the wheel, then add throttle' — not both at once.
